title: Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole

seo.title: Authentic Oaxacan Black Mole Recipe (Mole Negro)
seo.metaDescription: Master the rich flavors of Oaxacan Black Mole with our authentic recipe. Learn to make traditional Mole Negro, a complex Mexican sauce perfect for dinner. Get the recipe!
longTailKeywords: how to make Oaxacan Black Mole
Oaxacan Black Mole step by step
best Oaxacan Black Mole at home
seo.h1: Authentic Oaxacan Black Mole Recipe (Mole Negro)
h2Headings: Ingredients You'll Need for Oaxacan Black Mole
Pro Tips for the Best Oaxacan Black Mole
Serving Suggestions for Your Mole Negro
seo.imageAlt: A rich, dark bowl of authentic Oaxacan Black Mole (Mole Negro) served with chicken and rice, garnished with sesame seeds.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the deep, complex flavors of traditional Oaxacan Black Mole, a culinary masterpiece from Oaxaca, Mexico. Perfect for a memorable dinner!

description: Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole is a masterpiece of Mexican cuisine, a rich and complex sauce boasting a deep, smoky flavor from various chiles, nuts, spices, and a hint of chocolate. This traditional dish from Oaxaca is a true labor of love, offering layers of savory and subtly sweet notes that perfectly complement chicken or pork. Prepare for an unforgettable culinary journey to the heart of Mexico with every spoonful.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chiles
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of lard or oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Briefly toast the dried chiles (Ancho, Mulato, Pasilla, Arbol) for 30 seconds per side until fragrant but not burnt. Remove and immediately soak them in hot water for 20-30 minutes until softened. Reserve the soaking liquid. In the same skillet, char the tomatoes, tomatillos, onion, and garlic until softened and slightly browned. Set aside.

instructions.title: Toast Nuts and Spices
instructions.text: In the same skillet, add another tablespoon of lard or oil. Toast the almonds, pecans, pumpkin seeds, and sesame seeds until lightly golden and fragrant. Be careful not to burn them. Remove from skillet. Next, toast the raisins, cinnamon stick, cloves, peppercorns, cumin seeds, oregano, and thyme for about 1 minute until aromatic. Set aside with the nuts and seeds.

instructions.title: Fry Plantain and Tortillas
instructions.text: Add the remaining tablespoon of lard or oil to the skillet. Fry the sliced plantain until golden brown. Remove and set aside. Then, fry the torn corn tortillas until crisp. This adds body and richness to the mole. Remove and set aside.

instructions.title: Blend the Mole Paste
instructions.text: Drain the softened chiles, reserving the soaking liquid. In a blender, combine the softened chiles, charred vegetables (tomatoes, tomatillos, onion, garlic), toasted nuts and spices, fried plantain, and fried tortillas. Add about 1 cup of chicken broth and 1/2 cup of the reserved chile soaking liquid. Blend until very smooth, adding more broth or soaking liquid as needed to achieve a thick, pourable paste. This may require blending in batches.

instructions.title: Cook the Mole Sauce
instructions.text: Pass the blended mole paste through a fine-mesh sieve into a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven. This step is crucial for a smooth, velvety texture. Discard any solids left in the sieve. Add the remaining chicken broth to the strained mole. Bring to a simmer over medium heat, stirring frequently to prevent sticking. Add the Mexican chocolate and stir until it completely dissolves. Season with salt to taste, and a pinch of sugar if desired to balance the flavors.

instructions.title: Simmer and Serve
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to low and continue to simmer the mole for at least 30-45 minutes, stirring occasionally. The sauce should thicken and deepen in color and flavor. If it becomes too thick, add a little more chicken broth or water. Serve the Mole Negro hot over cooked chicken, turkey, or pork, with a side of Mexican rice and warm corn tortillas. Garnish with a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds.

tips: Toasting the chiles properly is key for flavor development; watch them closely to avoid burning, which makes the mole bitter.
For an even richer flavor, use homemade chicken broth. If you cant find Mexican chocolate, use unsweetened cocoa powder with a pinch of sugar and cinnamon.

Mole Negro improves with age. Make it a day ahead and gently reheat it for even deeper, more integrated flavors. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days or freeze for up to 3 months.
article: Mole Negro Oaxacan Black Mole is a beloved Mexican Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
